qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, congratulations on closing Cirrus. Now that the deal is done, can you walk us through the updated integration milestones, specifically the timing for native Cirrus capabilities inside the Stratus console?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Aaron, thank you. With Cirrus now closed, we are reaffirming our prior commitment to deliver native Cirrus query capabilities inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026. Engineering integration is tracking to plan and we will have a customer-facing beta available in early Q4. The general availability target remains end of fiscal Q4 2026.

qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, on the Stratus Copilot product. You committed last quarter to general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026. The early feedback from your user conference seemed positive. Are you still on track for that GA date?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Theo, I want to be straightforward with you. We are not going to hit the end of fiscal Q4 2026 GA target for Stratus Copilot that we committed to last quarter. Based on feedback we received at the user conference, we are making meaningful architectural changes to the agent runtime so that we can offer stronger enterprise data-isolation guarantees by default. We now expect Stratus Copilot to reach general availability in fiscal Q2 2027 rather than the end of fiscal Q4 2026 timing we previously shared. We considered shipping on the original timeline with a more limited feature set but concluded that the enterprise-readiness work was non-negotiable.

qa_pairs.question_text: Helena, can you help us bridge the change in the GAAP operating income outlook? Specifically how much of the swing is one-time deal costs versus ongoing run-rate stock-based compensation from the Cirrus team?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Marcia, of the roughly 65 million dollar swing relative to our prior framework, approximately 25 million is one-time deal and integration cost that will not repeat. The remaining 40 million is ongoing run-rate dilution from Cirrus stock-based compensation and intangible amortisation. The intangible amortisation steps down meaningfully in fiscal 2028 as the acquired technology amortises out. We expect to grow back into GAAP operating profitability during fiscal 2027.

qa_pairs.question_text: On EMEA enterprise. Last quarter you guided EMEA back to mid-single-digit year-over-year growth by fiscal Q4 2026. Has the trajectory held up in Q3?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Sandra, the trajectory has been better than we expected. EMEA net new ARR in Q3 was up 38 percent quarter over quarter and roughly flat year over year, which is a meaningful improvement from the down 5 percent we saw in Q2. We now expect EMEA to return to positive year-over-year growth in Q4 and we are increasingly confident that mid-single-digit growth is achievable.

qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, beyond Cirrus, can you talk about your inorganic strategy going forward? Should investors expect more M and A in fiscal 2027?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Jens, our default posture is organic. The Cirrus opportunity was unique because of the technology and team fit. We have nothing else of comparable size on the radar. We continue to evaluate small technology tuck-ins to fill specific product gaps, but I would not encourage you to model a meaningful M and A spend for fiscal 2027 at this stage.

qa_pairs.question_text: Helena, you have now spent 285 million dollars of the 500 million dollar buyback authorisation through three quarters. Is the Board considering an expanded authorisation given the cash flow strength?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Yuki, as we said at the start of the year we will revisit the authorisation level at the beginning of fiscal 2027. With the Cirrus capital deployment we want to rebuild a comfortable cash buffer before we commit to an expanded program. We will share our updated capital-return framework on the Q4 call.

qa_pairs.question_text: Daniel, last question on competitive dynamics. Are you seeing any change in pricing behaviour from the hyperscaler-native observability tools, and how is your win rate trending?
qa_pairs.answer_text: Wendy, win rates against hyperscaler-native tools held above 75 percent again this quarter in tracked competitive deals. We have seen one of the hyperscalers introduce a more aggressive discounting motion for greenfield workloads, but it has not materially changed our enterprise consolidation deals where customers value the multi-cloud single pane of glass. We will continue to monitor closely.

commitments.source_quote: we are reaffirming our prior commitment to deliver native Cirrus query capabilities inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.

commitments.commitment_text: Return to GAAP operating profitability during fiscal 2027.

commitments.commitment_text: Continue to fully exhaust the 500 million dollar share repurchase authorisation by the end of fiscal 2026.
commitments.source_quote: we continue to expect to fully exhaust the 500 million dollar authorisation by the end of fiscal 2026.

commitments.commitment_text: Share an updated capital-return framework on the Q4 fiscal 2026 earnings call.

reconciliation_targets.q2_commitment_text: Close the Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026.
reconciliation_targets.q2_source_quote: we expect to close the previously announced Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026, which will materially expand our footprint in real-time log analytics.
reconciliation_targets.q3_evidence_quote: closing the Cirrus Analytics acquisition on schedule in the final week of fiscal Q3, exactly as we committed on the Q2 call.

reconciliation_targets.q2_commitment_text: Full-year fiscal 2026 non-GAAP operating margin will land at approximately 19 percent.
reconciliation_targets.q2_source_quote: we now expect full-year non-GAAP operating margin to land at approximately 19 percent, up from our prior guidance of 18 percent.
reconciliation_targets.q3_evidence_quote: putting us on track to deliver our full-year fiscal 2026 non-GAAP operating margin at approximately 19 percent, consistent with the updated guidance we provided last quarter.

reconciliation_targets.q2_commitment_text: Deliver positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026.
reconciliation_targets.q2_source_quote: We also expect to deliver positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026, which would be a first for Nimbus.
reconciliation_targets.q3_evidence_quote: we now expect GAAP operating income to be slightly negative for fiscal 2026, in the range of negative 25 to negative 40 million dollars. We did not achieve that commitment and we want to be transparent about that.

reconciliation_targets.q2_commitment_text: Launch Stratus Copilot into general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.
reconciliation_targets.q2_source_quote: we plan to launch our Stratus Copilot product into general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026.
reconciliation_targets.q3_evidence_quote: We now expect Stratus Copilot to reach general availability in fiscal Q2 2027 rather than the end of fiscal Q4 2026 timing we previously shared.
